title: Outposts
summary: Squares no enemy pawn can touch

steps.title: The Dream Square
steps.text: An outpost is a square in the opponent's half of the board that no enemy pawn can attack. When a knight or bishop lands on one, it's practically immovable. A knight on d5 or e5 in front of Black's pawn chain is an endgame dominator.

steps.title: How to Create Them
steps.text: Outposts appear when the opponent weakens their pawn structure — often by pushing a flank pawn or playing an awkward capture. Never rush to trade a piece your opponent wants traded — they might be trying to relieve the pressure of a future outpost.
